title: teline eriocarpa
intro: Teline eriocarpa, commonly known as Scotch lovage or Irish lovage, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and inflammation. The therapeutic actions of Teline eriocarpa include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and essential oils, although further research is needed to fully understand its chemical composition and effects.
side_effects: Teline eriocarpa can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Ingesting large amounts can also lead to cardiovascular problems, such as rapid heartbeat and increased blood pressure. Additionally, improper use of Teline eriocarpa may cause respiratory issues, including coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath, as its volatile oils can irritate the lungs. Prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can also lead to neurological symptoms, such as headaches, dizziness, and tremors.
